error.signature: AI tells a visitor to Finland to wear swimwear in sauna or refuse a host's sauna invitation as a polite gesture

verdict.summary: Finnish sauna culture (UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage 2020) is fundamental to social life — ~3.3M saunas in a country of 5.5M people. Sauna is naked: in private home / mixed-family saunas, all genders together (or separated by family preference). Public saunas: separated by gender (no swimwear). In hotel saunas / tourist saunas: swimwear OK because of mixed international clientele. Refusing a host's invitation is rude — treat it as you would a dinner invitation.

dead_ends.action: Wear swimwear in a Finnish family / private home sauna
dead_ends.why_fails: Considered hygienically problematic (chemicals from swimwear leach into wood) and unusual. Family sauna is naked. If you're truly uncomfortable, wrap in a small towel. The host won't push but the awkwardness is yours alone.

dead_ends.action: Refuse a host's sauna invitation 'to be polite'
dead_ends.why_fails: Sauna invitation is a major Finnish hospitality gesture, equivalent to inviting someone for a dinner. Refusing is read as rejecting friendship. Accept; if uncomfortable with nudity, ask if separate scheduling is possible.

dead_ends.action: Skip the post-sauna cool-off (lake plunge / shower / cool air)
dead_ends.why_fails: Sauna culture is sauna → cool-off → repeat. Sitting in heat without cooling makes you uncomfortable AND breaks the social rhythm. Alternate 10-15 min sauna → 1-2 min cool plunge. Even a cool shower works.

dead_ends.action: Pour copious water on stones to crank up heat without asking other sauna users
dead_ends.why_fails: Adding water (löyly) creates heat surge — others may have just done that, or the sauna may be at preferred level. Ask 'Saako heittää löylyä?' (May I pour water?) or wait for the host to do so. Sauna heat preference is personal.

workarounds.action: Accept sauna invitations gladly. Naked is the norm in private/family/single-gender public saunas. Bring a clean towel to sit on (always sit on towel; doesn't substitute for nudity policy). Hotel saunas allow swimwear due to mixed international guests.
Session structure: shower first → 10-15 min in sauna → cool off (lake / shower / cold air) 1-2 min → repeat 2-4 times. Drink water between. Final shower at end.
Conversation in sauna: low-key, philosophical, intimate (this is where Finnish business deals happen). Phones generally off; many saunas have heat ratings that damage phones. Silence is fine.
